articles.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
articles.summary: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

articles.title: The Iran War Sent Shock Waves Through Asia That Are Likely to Spread
articles.summary: The Asia-Pacific was hit hard and quick by the war in Iran and its energy bottlenecks. Scenes of crisis there indicate that problems are multiplying and spreading.

articles.title: U.K police investigating if attacks in London are the work of Iranian proxies
articles.summary: U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.

articles.title: U.S. Military Strikes a Boat in the Caribbean, Killing 3
articles.summary: The latest attack raised the death toll to at least 180 in the campaign by the United States against people it accuses of smuggling drugs at sea.

articles.title: Communities launch cleanup after severe weather and tornadoes churn across Midwest
articles.summary: At least 66 tornado reports were submitted across multiple states on Friday, according to the National Weather Service.

articles.title: Democrats Eye a Broader Battlefield to Capture Congress in November
articles.summary: Weighed down by President Trump’s approval ratings, some Republican incumbents are struggling to raise money while Democrats look for targets like a Tennessee seat south of Nashville.

articles.title: U.S. says it seized an Iranian cargo ship, ceasefire expires soon
articles.summary: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.

articles.title: Will the DHS shutdown affect security for the World Cup?
articles.summary: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the t

articles.title: Muslim Southerners Face a Fresh Wave of Hateful Political Rhetoric
articles.summary: Some Muslim voters were once drawn to Republican positions on family values and individual liberty, but as Southern politicians stoke anti-Islamic sentiment, many feel threatened.

articles.title: Trump Administration Demand for Ballot Info Rebuffed by Michigan
articles.summary: The Department of Justice is seeking ballots and other materials from the 2024 election. Michigan officials call it election interference.

articles.title: 8 children killed in a shooting in Louisiana, police say
articles.summary: The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.

articles.title: Syrian Billionaires Needed a Favor in Washington. They Invoked the Trump Name.
articles.summary: The attempt by the Khayyats to influence foreign policy while discussions are underway about potential Trump family deals is an increasingly common feature of the president’s second term.

articles.title: Americans Living Abroad for Lower Costs Now Say Returning Home Is Too Expensive
articles.summary: Americans have enjoyed lifestyles that would normally be out of reach to them by working remotely in countries with lower living costs.

articles.title: Why nearly every farmer who grows these chile peppers is a woman
articles.summary: Chile peppers are a traditional part of Indian cuisine — and a key crop for women farmers. They say it's too demanding for men. "In spite of the challenges," says one, "we've found freedom."

articles.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

articles.title: Real estate investors are buying up long-term care facilities. Residents can suffer
articles.summary: Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.

articles.title: Are Democrats Becoming a Party of Tax Cuts?
articles.summary: As Democrats try to find a way back to power in Washington, some see tax cuts as a quick and easy way to address affordability. The wonks are freaking out.

articles.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
articles.summary: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

articles.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
articles.summary: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

articles.title: Life jacket worn by a passenger who survived the Titanic auctioned off for over $900,000
articles.summary: A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.

articles.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
articles.summary: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
